text.title: java-jpa-hibernate Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for java-jpa-hibernate as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Lombok @Data on JPA entities: generates equals/hashCode over all fields including lazy collections, causing infinite recursion and broken Set semantics.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: @Data iterates all fields for equals; lazy collection fields trigger a DB load and form a cycle. Use @Getter @Setter only and implement equals/hashCode manually on a business key.

failure-mode.description: FetchType.EAGER on @OneToMany or @ManyToMany to "be safe": every query loads all associated records, creating Cartesian explosions when multiple collections are fetched.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: EAGER fetch cannot be selectively disabled at the query level; once declared on the mapping it applies to every find. It multiplies rows when joined with other EAGER collections, hitting MultipleBagFetchException.

failure-mode.description: cascade = CascadeType.ALL on a @ManyToOne relationship: deleting the child cascades to the parent, wiping the parent entity and all its other children.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: CascadeType.ALL on @ManyToOne propagates persist/merge/remove up the ownership chain — semantically backwards. Cascade is appropriate on @OneToMany from parent to children, not from child to parent.

failure-mode.description: Changing an entity column type or renaming a column without a paired Flyway migration.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hibernate schema validation (spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto=validate) will throw a SchemaManagementException on startup. Production deployments without migrations cause downtime or data loss on column type changes.

failure-mode.description: @Modifying on a bulk UPDATE without clearAutomatically = true: subsequent findById returns stale entity from first-level cache.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Bulk UPDATE bypasses Hibernate's entity lifecycle; the first-level cache (L1) is not aware of the change and returns the pre-update state. clearAutomatically flushes the L1 cache after the bulk operation.

failure-mode.description: Using @SpringBootTest for repository slice tests instead of @DataJpaTest.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: @SpringBootTest loads the full application context including web layer, security, and services. @DataJpaTest slices to just the JPA layer, boots 10x faster, and isolates schema issues from application logic.

failure-mode.description: LazyInitializationException in controllers: entity with a lazy association is serialized outside the Hibernate session after the transaction closes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: OSIV (open-in-view) masks this problem in development by keeping the session open. With OSIV disabled (the correct setting), agents must use DTO projections or JOIN FETCH to load required associations within the transaction.

failure-mode.description: LLM emits `@Data` (Lombok) on entities — generates `equals`/`hashCode` over all fields including collections, causing infinite loops and broken `Set` semantics.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening

failure-mode.description: LLM defaults to `FetchType.EAGER` "to be safe" — kills perf.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening

failure-mode.description: Agent adds `cascade = CascadeType.ALL` everywhere, including on `@ManyToOne` (semantic nonsense).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening

failure-mode.description: `@Transactional` on `private` methods is a no-op (proxy). Self-invocation also breaks.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening

failure-mode.description: LLM tends to skip the migration when changing entity columns.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mandate: every entity diff requires a migration diff.

failure-mode.description: Generated tests use `@SpringBootTest` even for repository slices — slow.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening

failure-mode.description: Human checkpoint: any schema change touching production data (rename, type change, drop column) requires migration plan review (expand-contract pattern).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
